coin_profile.name: BNB
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: BNB is the native token of the BNB Chain (formerly Binance Smart Chain) and the Binance exchange ecosystem. It offers reduced trading fees on Binance, powers the BNB Chain network, and undergoes quarterly token burns. BNB benefits from Binance's position as the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange.

BNB.name: BNB

moat.rationale: BNB possesses a moderate moat anchored by strong ecosystem lock-in (BSC dominance in certain markets, exchange listing requirements) and cash-flow capture through exchange fees and token burns, but faces significant competitive pressures: BSC can be forked (and has been), network effects are weaker than L1 settlement layers, regulatory uncertainty around Binance undermines long-term defensibility, and token utility relies heavily on exchange-specific mechanics rather than protocol-inherent demand. The 'network demand' score of 1 is particularly damaging—BNB lacks organic user growth drivers independent of Binance's business expansion.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.

forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: DeFiLlama has no fee data for this token. This typically means the protocol does not generate trackable on-chain fees, or its fee reporting is not yet indexed. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

coin_profile.name: Bitcoin
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Store of Value asset is a cryptocurrency whose main purpose is to hold and protect your wealth over time — the same way people have used gold for thousands of years. It does not earn fees from users. Instead, its value comes from scarcity, trust, and the belief that it will be worth more in the future. Bitcoin is the most well-known example. We value these assets by comparing them to gold and looking at the cost of producing new coins.
coin_profile.description: Bitcoin is the original decentralised digital currency and the world's largest cryptocurrency by market cap. It operates on a proof-of-work blockchain with a fixed supply of 21 million coins, making it a programmatically scarce store of value. Often referred to as 'digital gold', Bitcoin is primarily used as a long-term store of value and hedge against inflation.

moat.rationale: Bitcoin's protocol moat is unmatched: 15+ years of proven security, immutable 21M supply cap (dilution_control: 10), dominant hash rate concentration creating insurmountable 51% attack costs, and network effects where each participant strengthens monetary credibility. While technically forkable, successful forks (BCH, BSV) have failed to replicate BTC's value accrual, demonstrating that BTC's moat transcends code—it's anchored in first-mover monetary premium (8/10), institutional adoption lock-in, and role as macro store of value where switching costs are existential for holders.

Supply Adjusted Value did not fire: FDV/MC is within acceptable range, so no supply dilution penalty was applied. Network Activity (NVT) treats volume as monetary circulation velocity — higher NVT targets apply to SoV assets held long-term rather than traded for fees. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

moat.rationale: BCH has minimal competitive defensibility as a forked Bitcoin variant with no differentiation—users can switch to BTC, LTC, or any other UTXO chain with negligible friction, and the fork itself proved the moat is easily replicated. Network effects are weak and declining (rank 18, adoption momentum score 5), with no unique value capture for token holders beyond speculative appreciation, while ecosystem lock-in is near-zero given its primary use case (payments) is better served by BTC, Stablecoin L2s, or traditional rails. The regulatory resilience benefit of decentralization is offset by BCH's complete lack of compelling technical or economic moat—making it vulnerable to displacement by any faster or more established payment layer.

bear_points: <cite index="1-11">Binance's shift to standard trading fees for BFUSD pairs starting January 9, 2026 could reduce trading volumes and suppress near-term price appreciation as fee friction increases for retail traders</cite>.

bull_points: <cite index="3-30">The ecosystem trend toward tokenized on-chain dollars moving from experimental pilots to enterprise financial infrastructure—inside treasury workflows, cross-border settlement, and programmable B2B payments—creates a multi-year tailwind for BFUSD's underlying use case</cite>.

coin_profile.name: Bitcoin SV
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Store of Value asset is a cryptocurrency whose main purpose is to hold and protect your wealth over time — the same way people have used gold for thousands of years. It does not earn fees from users. Instead, its value comes from scarcity, trust, and the belief that it will be worth more in the future. Bitcoin is the most well-known example. We value these assets by comparing them to gold and looking at the cost of producing new coins.
coin_profile.description: Bitcoin SV (BSV) — a Store of Value ranked #150 by market cap.

BSV.name: Bitcoin SV

dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: BSV lacks meaningful competitive defensibility—it was forked from Bitcoin Cash and offers no technical differentiation that prevents further forking or competition from superior L1s. Network effects are severely hampered by minimal developer adoption (rank 150, $0.3B market cap) and negligible ecosystem lock-in; users and capital can trivially migrate to Bitcoin, Ethereum, or other Store of Value alternatives with stronger security premises and liquidity. While BSV mimics Bitcoin's scarcity (8/10) and dilution control (10/10), it captures zero token utility for holders, lacks settlement-layer necessity, and faces existential regulatory uncertainty due to its contentious origins and association with disputed Bitcoin Cash politics—creating moat erosion rather than defensibility.

coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.8/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 33.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
